metaTitle: Follow Up Boss vs AgentStack | Real Estate CRM Comparison (2026)
metaDescription: Follow Up Boss vs AgentStack — compare public pricing, AI inclusion, onboarding, and ownership before you choose your next real estate CRM.

hero.subhead: Follow Up Boss is a mature real estate CRM with strong team workflows. AgentStack takes a more operator-owned approach: transparent plans, built-in AI follow-up, and a full business operating system designed around one real estate brand at a time.

pullQuote.text: Follow Up Boss is well known for lead routing and team collaboration. AgentStack is built for operators who want the same day-to-day momentum plus more ownership, more built-in automation, and less per-user pricing drag.
pullQuote.author: The AgentStack team

painPoints.heading: Where operators start to feel the difference

bullets.title: Per-user pricing grows with the team
bullets.body: Follow Up Boss publishes user-based pricing, so the bill rises as you add agents. AgentStack's core plans are built around operating the business, not paying a new CRM tax every time headcount changes.

bullets.title: Calling is still an add-on on the entry plan
bullets.body: Follow Up Boss includes AI features on its pricing page, but notes that some of them rely on the paid Calling add-on on Grow. AgentStack ships web chat, SMS follow-up, and voice-ready workflows as part of the product offer.

bullets.title: You are still buying a CRM, not your operating layer
bullets.body: Follow Up Boss is excellent at organizing leads. AgentStack is designed to pair that CRM discipline with onboarding checklists, owned workflows, and an operator-first setup that turns the business process itself into the product.

advantages.title: Pricing stays simpler as the team scales
advantages.body: AgentStack's published plans are straightforward and brand-led. You pick the tier that fits the business model, not a per-seat math problem every time the roster changes.

advantages.title: AI is part of the workflow, not an edge case
advantages.body: Instant web chat, SMS follow-up, and guided next actions are built into AgentStack's core operating flow so the team always knows what should happen next.

advantages.title: The onboarding path mirrors the business
advantages.body: Build, Connect, Capture, Respond — AgentStack's checklist is designed around how agents actually launch. That means fewer blank screens and less guessing about what to configure first.

advantages.title: Everything points toward one real estate operating system
advantages.body: Lead capture, pipeline movement, scheduling, reviews, IDX, and add-ons sit in one coherent motion. You are not stitching together one CRM, one chat layer, and a stack of outside services just to stay responsive.

rows.label: Built-in AI follow-up in the product story
Calling required on entry plan for stronger AI outcomes
IDX positioning on public marketing pages

pricing.heading: Pricing compared honestly

leadstack.headline: Published plans starting at $199/month
leadstack.detail: AgentStack publishes clear tiered pricing for Solo, Team, and Broker use cases, with AI follow-up already central to the offer and no separate CRM seat math for every new agent.
notes: AI web chat, SMS follow-up, and the onboarding system are part of the product direction from day one.

competitor.headline: From $69/user/month on Grow
competitor.detail: Follow Up Boss publicly lists monthly and annual pricing, says there are no contracts, and includes AI features in the offer — with the important caveat that some AI features depend on the paid Calling add-on on Grow.
notes: Grow: $69 per user per month, with Calling as a $39 per user add-on on the monthly plan.
Grow annual pricing is listed at $58 per user per month, with Calling as a $33 per user add-on.
Pro: $499/month including 10 users, with additional users at $49/month.
Platform: $1000/month including 30 users, with additional users at $20/month.

pricing.summary: Follow Up Boss is much more transparent than many real estate CRMs, which we respect. AgentStack's edge is not that FUB hides pricing — it's that AgentStack turns the CRM into a more complete operating system with a clearer real-estate-specific launch path.

competitorWins.heading: Where Follow Up Boss deserves credit
bullets: Their pricing page is unusually clear for this category.
They explicitly advertise setup, training, guided onboarding, and seven-day-a-week support.
For teams already bought into the FUB way of working, the learning curve is low.
competitorWins.closing: If you want a proven CRM with clear pricing and strong team features, Follow Up Boss is a serious option. AgentStack is the better fit when you want the CRM plus the operating method around it.

items.question: Is this saying Follow Up Boss is bad?
items.answer: No. Follow Up Boss is one of the more straightforward real estate CRM products to evaluate publicly. This page is about fit: FUB is a CRM with strong routing and collaboration; AgentStack is trying to be the operating system wrapped around the real estate workflow.

items.question: What is the biggest difference for an agent or team lead?
items.answer: The biggest difference is how much of the operating process is guided for you. AgentStack is built around the setup path, AI follow-up, recommended next action, and related systems that keep the business moving even when you are with clients.

items.question: Does Follow Up Boss publish pricing and contract terms?
items.answer: Yes. Their live pricing page states "Simple pricing, free trial, no contracts" and lists monthly and annual pricing tiers.

items.question: Does Follow Up Boss include AI?
items.answer: Their pricing page says AI features are included, and also notes that some AI features use data from the paid Calling add-on on the Grow plan.

items.question: Who should choose AgentStack instead?
items.answer: Choose AgentStack if you want a CRM decision that also solves launch sequence, AI follow-up, IDX positioning, lead capture systems, and the operator workflow around the CRM instead of stopping at lead organization.

finalCta.headline: Choose the CRM that also moves the business forward
finalCta.body: If you want transparent pricing and a stronger real-estate operating layer on top of the CRM itself, AgentStack is built for that job.

verification.heading: What we verified directly on the official sites
verification.intro: These are the buyer-facing details we could confirm from the live official pages on July 16, 2026 — no rumor, no third-party review summaries.

items.label: Pricing
items.leadstack: Published on the marketing site: Solo $199/mo, Team $299/mo, Broker $399/mo.
items.competitor: Published by Follow Up Boss: Grow starts at $69/user/month on the monthly plan, Pro is $499/month including 10 users, and Platform is $1000/month including 30 users.

items.label: Contract terms
items.leadstack: Monthly or annual pricing with self-serve public plans.
items.competitor: The Follow Up Boss pricing page explicitly says: "Simple pricing, free trial, no contracts."

items.label: AI inclusion
items.leadstack: AI follow-up is part of the core marketing promise across web chat, SMS, and the broader operating flow.
items.competitor: The Follow Up Boss pricing page says AI features are included, while noting that some AI features rely on data from the paid Calling add-on on Grow.

items.label: Setup path
items.leadstack: A public method-led launch path is shown on the marketing site: Build, Connect, Capture, Respond.
items.competitor: Follow Up Boss publicly advertises setup and training, help importing contacts, help connecting lead sources, guided onboarding, and personalized onboarding on higher tiers.
